description.en: Question 1. Which statement is true about the values of the two expressions below? Expression A: 3 × (8 + 4). Expression B: 8 + 4. Choices: A: The value of Expression B is three times the value of Expression A. B: The value of Expression A is three times the value of Expression B. C: The value of Expression A is three more than the value of Expression B. D: The value of Expression B is three more than the value of Expression A.

en: A: The value of Expression B is three times the value of Expression A
B: The value of Expression A is three times the value of Expression B
C: The value of Expression A is three more than the value of Expression B
D: The value of Expression B is three more than the value of Expression A

description.en: Question 2. Which diagram represents a volume of one cubic unit? Choices: A: A two-dimensional square measuring 1 unit high and 1 unit wide. B: A two-dimensional square measuring 2 units high and 2 units wide. C: A three-dimensional rectangular prism measuring 2 units high, 2 units wide, and 2 units deep; dashed lines indicate the hidden edges. D: A three-dimensional cube measuring 1 unit high, 1 unit wide, and 1 unit deep; dashed lines indicate the hidden edges.

en: 2 units high, 2 units wide, and 2 units deep
1 unit high, 1 unit wide, and 1 unit deep
A: A two-dimensional square measuring 1 unit high and 1 unit wide
B: A two-dimensional square measuring 2 units high and 2 units wide
C: A three-dimensional rectangular prism measuring 2 units high, 2 units wide, and 2 units deep; dashed lines indicate the hidden edges
D: A three-dimensional cube measuring 1 unit high, 1 unit wide, and 1 unit deep; dashed lines indicate the hidden edges

description.en: Question 5. Which phrase describes the volume of a 3-dimensional figure? Choices: A: the number of square units it takes to fill a solid figure. B: the number of cubic units it takes to fill a solid figure. C: the number of square units it takes to cover the outside of a solid figure. D: the number of cubic units it takes to cover the outside of a solid figure.

en: A: the number of square units it takes to fill a solid figure
B: the number of cubic units it takes to fill a solid figure
C: the number of square units it takes to cover the outside of a solid figure
D: the number of cubic units it takes to cover the outside of a solid figure

description.en: Question 7. A recipe for 1 batch of muffins included 2/3 cup of raisins. Ina made 2 1/2 batches of muffins. How many cups of raisins did she use? Choices: A: 1 4/6. B: 1 5/6. C: 2 2/6. D: 3 1/6.
Question 9. Mani, James, and Isidro equally shared 1/2 of a pie. Which fraction of the whole pie did each of them receive? Choices: A: 1/6. B: 1/5. C: 2/3. D: 3/2.

Question 10. A number is given below: 136.25. In a different number, the 6 represents a value which is one-tenth of the value of the 6 in the number above. What value is represented by the 6 in the other number? Choices: A: six hundredths. B: six tenths. C: six ones. D: six tens.

Question 13. Austin collected 30 9/10 kilograms of glass for recycling. Exactly 2/3 of the glass he collected was blue. What was the total amount, in kilograms, of blue glass Austin collected? Choices: A: 20 3/5. B: 27 2/3. C: 30 3/5. D: 30 11/13.
Question 14. What number goes in the blank to make the statement below true? 3,840 ounces = _____ pounds. Choices: A: 24. B: 240. C: 480. D: 61,440.

Question 16. What is the area, in square inches, of a rectangle with the dimensions shown in the diagram below? The rectangle measures 7/8 inch in length and 3/16 inch in width. Choices: A: 21/128. B: 3/14. C: 10/24. D: 24/112.
Question 18. What is the value of the expression below? 1,536 ÷ 24. Choices: A: 57. B: 64. C: 65. D: 68.

Question 19. What is the volume, in cubic centimeters, of the figure below? The figure is a rectangular prism made of equal unit cubes. Its front face is 5 cubes wide and 3 cubes high, and it is 3 cubes deep. A separate key shows one cube labeled “1 cubic centimeter.” Choices: A: 15. B: 24. C: 30. D: 45.

Question 23. Millie designed a rectangular label to put on the front of her scrapbook. The label was 5/12 foot wide and 5/6 foot long. What was the area, in square feet, of the label? Choices: A: 2 6/12. B: 1 3/12. C: 10/18. D: 25/72.
Question 27. Which expression means the same as the phrase below? Subtract 3 from the product of 8 and 5. Choices: A: (5 × 8) + 3. B: (5 × 8) − 3. C: 5 × (8 − 3). D: 5 × (8 + 3).

Question 32. Jim gave the following description of a figure: It is a quadrilateral. All sides are equal in length. There are two equal obtuse angles and two equal acute angles. Which figure could match Jim’s description? Choices: A: rectangle. B: rhombus. C: square. D: pentagon.
Question 33. Which expression is equivalent to 100,000? Choices: A: 10⁴. B: 10⁵. C: 10⁶. D: 10⁷.

Question 34. Lincoln had 2 books in his backpack. One book had a mass of 3 pounds 7 ounces, and the other book had a mass of 2 pounds 10 ounces. What was the total mass, in ounces, of the books? Choices: A: 60. B: 77. C: 80. D: 97.
Question 36. A box contains 512 grams of cereal. One serving of cereal is 56 grams. How many servings of cereal does the box contain? Choices: A: 9 1/4. B: 9 1/8. C: 9 8/56. D: 9 8/512.

Question 38. Lori and Maria bought juice to make fruit punch. Maria bought 5 bottles of juice, each containing 750 milliliters. Lori bought 4 liters of juice. Based on this information, which sentence is true? Choices: A: Lori bought 0.25 liter more juice than Maria. B: Maria bought 0.75 liter more juice than Lori. C: Maria bought 33.5 liters more juice than Lori. D: Lori bought 36.25 liters more juice than Maria.

en: A: Lori bought 0.25 liter more juice than Maria
B: Maria bought 0.75 liter more juice than Lori
C: Maria bought 33.5 liters more juice than Lori
D: Lori bought 36.25 liters more juice than Maria

description.en: Question 39. Which decimal best represents the location of point X on the number line? The horizontal number line has arrows pointing in both directions and 11 equally spaced tick marks. The first tick is labeled 50/100, and the last tick is labeled 60/100. Point X is marked by a closed dot on the sixth tick from the left, five equal intervals to the right of 50/100. Choices: A: 0.5. B: 0.55. C: 0.56. D: 0.6.

Question 40. Clark made a model of his house. His house is 30 1/2 feet long. The dimensions of the model were 1/25 the dimensions of Clark’s actual home. What is the length, in feet, of the model? Choices: A: 1 10/50. B: 1 11/50. C: 30 23/50. D: 30 27/50.
Question 41. Deb has a board that measures 5 feet in length. How many 1/4-foot-long pieces can Deb cut from the board? Choices: A: 1. B: 9. C: 10. D: 20.
Question 43. In which number does the 5 represent a value 10 times the value represented by the 5 in 35,187? Choices: A: 117,568. B: 247,351. C: 325,827. D: 453,362.
Question 44. Michele is 52 inches tall. Her father is 6 feet 3 inches tall. Exactly how many inches taller is Michele’s father than Michele? Choices: A: 11. B: 13. C: 23. D: 25.
Question 49. What is the value of 2/5 + 3/7? Choices: A: 6/35. B: 5/12. C: 6/12. D: 29/35.
